content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Roger Sanders 
Executive Director 
San Francisco County 
Democratic Party 
100 McAllister Street 
San Francisco, CA 
94102 
Dear Mr. Sanders: 
June 2, 1989 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-193 
You have requested advice regarding campaign disclosure 
requirements under the Political Reform Act (the "Act,,).l 
QUESTION 
Are Democratic clubs which are officially chartered by the 
San Francisco County Democratic Central Committee required to 
fulfill the identification and disclosure provisions for slate 
mailer organizations when the clubs send slate mailers? 
CONCLUSION 
If a Democratic club receives payments totaling $500 or more 
in a calendar year from any source for the purpose of having a 
specific candidate or specific candidates included in the slate 
mailer, the club will qualify as a slate mailer organization and 
must comply with the identification and disclosure provisions for 
slate mailer organizations. 
However, if the club pays for a slate 
mailer from general fundraising activity, it would not be 
considered a slate mailer organization. 
FACTS 
The San Francisco County Democratic Party Central Committee 
charters official Democratic clubs ("clubs"). 
These clubs may 
send slate mailers which support or oppose four or more candidates 
or measures. 
1Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to Title 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804..0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Roger Sanders 
Page Two 
ANALYSIS 
A slate mailer organization is an organization which is 
involved in the production of one or more slate mailers,2 
exercises control over the selection of the candidates and 
measures to be supported or opposed in the slate mailers, and 
receives or is promised $500 or more in a calendar year for the 
production of one or more slate mailers. 
(Sections 82048.4(a) and 
82048.3.) 
Notwithstanding the above, an official committee of any 
political party is not a slate mailer organization. 
(Section 
82048.4(b) (2).) 
The Commission has determined that "Democratic clubs are not 
official committees of the Democratic Party because they are not 
part of the state central committee and the county central 
committees that make up the official structure of the Democratic 
Party in California." 
(In re Willmarth (1976), 2 FPPC Opinions 
130, copy enclosed.) 
Therefore, a Democratic club could qualify 
as a slate mailer organization if it receives payments totaling 
$500 or more from or on behalf of candidates who wish to be 
included in a slate mailer. 
In that event, the club would be 
required to comply with the disclosure and disclaimer requirements 
for slate mailer organizations contained in sections 84108, 84218-
84220, and 84305.5. 
If you have any additional questions, please contact me at 
(916) 322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
By: 
Enclosure 
2A "slate mailer means a mass mailing which supports or 
opposes a total of four or more candidates or ballot measures. 
(Section 82048.3.) 


March 29, 1989 
John H. Larson, Chair 
Fair Political Practices commission 
428 J street #800 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Mr. Larson: 
Are disclaimers required on slate mailers that are 
produced by Democratic Clubs that are officially 
chartered by the San Francisco County Democratic 
Central Committee? 
Sincerely, 
~~~'S 
Roger Sanders 
Executive Director 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Roger Sanders 
Executive Director 
April 4, 1989 
San Francisco County Democratic Party 
100 McAllister Street, Suite 350 
San Francisco, CA 
94102 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-193 
Dear Mr. Sanders: 
Your letter requesting advice under the political Reform Act 
was received on March 31, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
Ve?;trulY your~). 
.'/ 
1

~ 
Jeanne Prltchard 
....J( '7 ,2 j 
Chief Technical Assistance 
~-v~ 
and Analysis Division 
JP:plh 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 •
